---
title: "Alex Newhouse"
subtitle: "Political violence · online extremism · computational social science"
description: "Alex Newhouse — computational political scientist at CU Boulder. Online extremism, political violence, NLP, networks, and causal inference."
pagetitle: "Alex Newhouse"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.hero-copy}
[On the academic job market · 2027–28]{.jobmarket-banner}

[PhD Candidate · Political Science · CU Boulder]{.hero-kicker}

<h1 class="hero-title">Alex Newhouse</h1>

::: {.hero-dek}
I study **how online communities turn into offline political violence** — using transformer NLP, social network analysis, and time-series causal inference.
:::

::: {.hero-summary}
I'm a computational political scientist, Humane Studies Fellow, and National Humanities Alliance Research Fellow. Before CU Boulder, I served as Deputy Director of the Center on Terrorism, Extremism & Counterterrorism and as an investigative consultant to the U.S. House Select Committee on January 6th.
:::

::: {.action-row}
[Download CV](cv.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}
[Job-market packet](jobmarket.html){.btn .btn-secondary}
[Email](mailto:alex.newhouse@colorado.edu){.btn .btn-secondary}
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=NLXqzpoAAAAJ){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::
:::

::: {.hero-portrait}
```{=html}
<img src="profpic.jpeg" alt="Alex Newhouse">
```
:::
:::

[Job market paper]{.section-eyebrow}

::: {.callout-jmp}
**"'The Happening Is Coming': Apocalyptic Rhetoric on 4chan Following Mass Casualty Attacks"**

A transformer classifier and intervention analysis of 11 years of 4chan /pol/ posts showing that online apocalypticism spikes after mass-casualty terrorist attacks — evidence of feedback loops between digital communities and offline violence. Presented at APSA 2025. PDF available on request.

[Read dissertation overview](dissertation.html){.btn .btn-primary} [Request paper](mailto:alex.newhouse@colorado.edu?subject=Job%20market%20paper%20request){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::

[Start here]{.section-eyebrow}

## What I work on

::: {.an-grid}

::: {.an-card}
### [Research](research.html)

Online-offline radicalization, neo-fascist networks, AI safety, and gaming as a site of political socialization.

[See research →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [Tools & Code](tools.html)

NLP classifiers, datasets, and reproducible pipelines for studying extremist text, networks, and online communities at scale.

[See tools →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### [Teaching](teaching.html)

Quantitative methods, computational social science, political violence, and original R tutorials for undergraduate methods students.

[See teaching →]{.an-card-cta}
:::

:::

[For hiring committees, policy teams, and collaborators]{.section-eyebrow}

## At a glance

::: {.an-grid}

::: {.an-card}
### Scholarly identity

Comparative politics and methodology scholar focused on decentralized political violence, platform affordances, and online collective action.

[Political violence]{.tag} [Extremism]{.tag} [Computational social science]{.tag}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### Technical depth

I build production-grade pipelines with transformer models, network analysis, interrupted time series, and large-scale social media data.

[NLP]{.tag} [Networks]{.tag} [Causal inference]{.tag}
:::

::: {.an-card}
### Applied impact

My work has supported federal investigations, gaming-platform trust & safety, media explainers, and policy-facing extremism research.

[Jan. 6 Committee]{.tag} [Gaming platforms]{.tag} [Policy translation]{.tag}
:::

:::

[Selected outputs]{.section-eyebrow}

## Selected work

- **Newhouse, A. & Kowert, R.** (2025). [Extremist Identity Creation Through Performative Infighting on Steam](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/psychology/articles/10.3389/fpsyg.2025.1586566/full). *Frontiers in Psychology.*
- **Kowert, R., Kilmer, E., & Newhouse, A.** (2024). [Taking it to the Extreme: Prevalence and Nature of Extremist Sentiment in Games](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/psychology/articles/10.3389/fpsyg.2024.1410620/full). *Frontiers in Psychology.*
- **Newhouse, A.** (2021). [The Threat is the Network: The Multi-Node Structure of Neo-Fascist Accelerationism](https://ctc.westpoint.edu/the-threat-is-the-network-the-multi-node-structure-of-neo-fascist-accelerationism/). *CTC Sentinel.*
- **McGuffie, K. & Newhouse, A.** (2020). [The Radicalization Risks of GPT-3 and Advanced Neural Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2009.06807). *arXiv.*

[Full publication list →](research.html)

[Evidence of reach]{.section-eyebrow}

## Impact

::: {.stats-strip}
::: {.stat-tile}
[\$1.38M]{.stat-num}
[Funding as PI]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-tile}
[52]{.stat-num}
[Students mentored]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-tile}
[276K]{.stat-num}
[Article reads]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-tile}
[3]{.stat-num}
[Courses as instructor]{.stat-label}
:::
:::

[Public engagement]{.section-eyebrow}

## Featured in

::: {.press-strip}
Washington Post · New York Times · NPR · BBC · Politico · Wired · Bloomberg · Meet the Press · Vice · Axios · FiveThirtyEight · ProPublica
:::

[Freshness signal]{.section-eyebrow}

## Recent

::: {.news-list}
- [2026-04-14]{.news-date} Successfully defended dissertation prospectus.
- [2025-10-01]{.news-date} Awarded a Humane Studies Fellowship to study polarization in LLMs.
- [2025-10-01]{.news-date} Began Research Fellowship with the National Humanities Alliance Humanities Workforce Program.
- [2025-09-01]{.news-date} Presented job-market paper at APSA 2025.
- [2025-08-01]{.news-date} New article in *Frontiers in Psychology* on extremist identity formation on Steam.
:::

[See more writing and updates →](posts.html)

```{=html}
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Alex Newhouse",
  "givenName": "Alex",
  "familyName": "Newhouse",
  "url": "https://alexbnewhouse.github.io",
  "image": "https://alexbnewhouse.github.io/profpic.jpeg",
  "jobTitle": "PhD Candidate in Political Science",
  "affiliation": {
    "@type": "CollegeOrUniversity",
    "name": "University of Colorado Boulder"
  },
  "alumniOf": [
    {"@type": "CollegeOrUniversity", "name": "Middlebury College"},
    {"@type": "CollegeOrUniversity", "name": "Middlebury Institute of International Studies"},
    {"@type": "CollegeOrUniversity", "name": "Georgia Institute of Technology"}
  ],
  "sameAs": [
    "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=NLXqzpoAAAAJ",
    "https://orcid.org/0009-0003-6346-9527",
    "https://github.com/alexbnewhouse",
    "https://linkedin.com/in/alexbnewhouse",
    "https://bsky.app/profile/alexbnewhouse.bsky.social"
  ],
  "knowsAbout": [
    "Political Violence",
    "Online Extremism",
    "Computational Social Science",
    "Natural Language Processing",
    "Social Network Analysis",
    "Causal Inference"
  ]
}
</script>
```
